SEARCH
Package com.krishcpatel.realm.economy.event

Class LedgerRecordedEvent

java.lang.Object
com.krishcpatel.realm.economy.event.LedgerRecordedEvent
All Implemented Interfaces:
RealmEvent
public final class LedgerRecordedEvent extends Object implements RealmEvent
Event published after a ledger entry has been committed to the database.

This allows modules to react to money movement (monitoring, alerts, analytics, anti-bug checks) without directly coupling to the transaction system.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    LedgerRecordedEvent(long ledgerId, String type, long amount, String fromUuid, String toUuid, MoneySource source, String reference, String reason, String actor)
    Creates a new ledger-recorded event.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    String
    actor()
    Returns the actor that initiated the transaction (player UUID, CONSOLE, SYSTEM).
    long
    amount()
    Returns the positive amount moved in this transaction.
    long
    createdAt()
    Timestamp when the event instance was created.
    String
    fromUuid()
    Returns the sender UUID for a transfer/burn, or null for mint.
    long
    ledgerId()
    Returns the database id of the committed ledger row.
    String
    reason()
    Returns an optional human-readable reason for the transaction.
    String
    reference()
    Returns an optional external reference id (shop id, team id, plot id, etc.).
    MoneySource
    source()
    Returns the subsystem that initiated the transaction.
    String
    toUuid()
    Returns the receiver UUID for a transfer/mint, or null for burn.
    String
    type()
    Returns the transaction type (TRANSFER, MINT, BURN, SET).

  • Constructor Details

    • LedgerRecordedEvent

      public LedgerRecordedEvent(long ledgerId, String type, long amount, String fromUuid, String toUuid, MoneySource source, String reference, String reason, String actor)
      Creates a new ledger-recorded event.
      Parameters:
      ledgerId - id of the committed ledger row
      type - transaction type (TRANSFER, MINT, BURN, SET, etc.)
      amount - positive amount moved
      fromUuid - sender UUID (nullable depending on type)
      toUuid - receiver UUID (nullable depending on type)
      source - subsystem source category
      reference - optional external reference id
      reason - optional human-readable reason
      actor - initiator identifier (player UUID, CONSOLE, SYSTEM)
